name: operator-local-opencode-devloop
description: Operator-grade local coding workflow using OpenCode in ~/dev/temp with a real file-edit-test-repair loop.

playbooks.name: Local OpenCode Two Sum Dev Loop

operator_scenarios.name: Local OpenCode Two Sum Dev Loop

steps.label: prepare_opencode_workspace
steps.prompt: Please prepare an OpenCode workspace in ~/dev/temp/con-bench-opencode-twosum by launching OpenCode there and preparing a paired local shell target for file and test work.

steps.label: create_code_and_tests
steps.prompt: Keep the OpenCode target prepared, but use its paired local shell target for the deterministic coding work: create a small Python two_sum implementation and a unittest-based test file in ~/dev/temp/con-bench-opencode-twosum, then run `python3 -m unittest -q`. Only touch the OpenCode pane if a blocking trust or continue prompt must be cleared first.

steps.label: repair_without_new_target
steps.prompt: Now break one unittest on purpose in the paired local shell. If the OpenCode pane is waiting at a trust, continue, or edit-approval prompt, clear it in the same target. Then repair the failing test in the same workspace pair without creating a new target, and rerun `python3 -m unittest -q`.

steps.label: summarize_target_reuse
steps.prompt: Summarize which pane or target you used for each step.
